title: The Visionary Mind
description: An exploration of Steve Jobs' early years and the founding of Apple.
transcript: Steve Jobs was born in 1955. He grew up in the Silicon Valley area. In 1976, he and Steve Wozniak started Apple in a garage. They wanted to make computers accessible to everyone. The Apple II was a huge success...

title: The Wilderness Years
description: Steve's departure from Apple, the founding of NeXT, and the rise of Pixar.
transcript: In 1985, Jobs was forced out of Apple. He didn't give up. He started NeXT, a high-end workstation company. He also bought Pixar. Pixar went on to create Toy Story, the first computer-animated feature film...

title: The Great Return
description: The story of Apple's near-bankruptcy and Steve's triumphant return.
transcript: By 1996, Apple was in trouble. They bought NeXT for its operating system. Jobs returned as an advisor, then interim CEO. He simplified the product line and introduced the iMac...

title: The Mobile Revolution
description: How the iPod and iPhone changed the world forever.
transcript: In 2001, Apple launched the iPod. It changed how we listen to music. Then in 2007, the iPhone was born. It wasn't just a phone; it was a computer in your pocket...

title: The Final Act
description: Steve's later years, his legacy, and the future of Apple.
transcript: Steve Jobs' final years were marked by incredible product launches like the iPad. He focused on the intersection of technology and the liberal arts. His legacy continues to inspire millions...
